channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Stop burning money in 2026: How to find and cancel your unneeded subscriptions easily
item.description: The new year often brings sticker shock. A glance at our bank statements and credit card bills shows just how much we spent during the holidays, serving as a painful reminder that with the festivities behind us, we should work on getting our expenditures under control.
item.dc:creator: Michael Grothaus, Fast Company

item.title: The 30 most exciting new shows of 2026: TechRadar's top TV picks on Netflix, Prime Video, HBO Max, and more
item.description: There'll be lots of great new TV shows this year – here are our top choices for early 2026, plus some to look out for in the months ahead.
item.dc:creator: Tom Power, TechRadar

item.title: Unannounced 32TB Seagate hard drive surfaces in Japan for photoshoot
item.description: Seagate’s unannounced 32TB IronWolf Pro hard drive surfaced in Japanese retail, marking a quiet shift of extreme capacity toward consumers.
item.dc:creator: Efosa Udinmwen, TechRadar

item.title: The 14 global trends that will shape the climate in 2026
item.description: The future of the energy transition is being shaped by President Donald Trump’s climate policy rollbacks that favor fossil fuels — but also the life support for renewables and batteries paradoxically coming from data center power demand.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Experts warn AI advances could lead to 200,000 banking jobs being cut this year
item.description: European banking workers could be at risk of redundancy due to AI after years of job cuts from store closures.
item.dc:creator: Craig Hale, TechRadar

item.title: European banks may lay off 200,000 due to AI
item.description: Over 200,000 banking jobs in Europe could disappear by 2030 as banks invest more in AI and close physical branches according to a new analysis by Morgan Stanley reported by the Financial Times. This is equivalent to around 10% of the workforce at 35 major banks.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: How to watch Hell's Kitchen online: stream Battle of the States, season 24 for FREE from anywhere
item.description: Want to watch Hell's Kitchen from anywhere? Stream every new twist as chefs face brutal challengers, fiery eliminations, and Ramsay's relentless pressure.
item.dc:creator: Krishi Chowdhary, TechRadar

item.title: Sunderland vs Man City live streams: How to watch Premier League 2025-26 from anywhere in the world
item.description: We'll show you how to watch Sunderland vs Man City live streams from anywhere in the world as Haaland and co look to continue their run of Premier League wins.
item.dc:creator: Adam Marshall, TechRadar

item.title: Wispr CEO: What a post-keyboard office might look like
item.description: Voice-to-text tools have been around for decades, yet there’s little sign that office workers are relying any less on their keyboards.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: The top 10 AI stories of 2025
item.description: It’s safe to say that AI was the story of 2025: It topped CIOs’ tech agendas; vendors rolled out a constant stream of new AI products and features; and IT professionals and other knowledge workers got down to the business of integrating it into their workflows.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: On a remote Alaskan glacier, a 73-year mission of recovery and respect
item.description: "The glacier is changing rapidly. We don't know how much time we have leftso we've really focused on expediting the recovery efforts," Carlos Colon said. The post On a remote Alaskan glacier, a 73-year mission of recovery and respect first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Terry Gerton, Federal News Network

item.title: Microsoft typosquatting scam swaps letters to steal logins
item.description: Phishing scammers use rnicrosoft.com domain to impersonate Microsoft by replacing "m" with "rn" in typosquatting attacks that steal login credentials.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Nvidia licenses Groq’s inferencing chip tech and hires its leaders
item.description: Nvidia has licensed intellectual property from inferencing chip designer Groq, and hired away some of its senior executives, but stopped short of an outright acquisition.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: SoftBank expands AI infrastructure ambitions with $4B DigitalBridge acquisition
item.description: SoftBank Group has announced plans to acquire DigitalBridge Group, an asset manager specializing in digital infrastructure investments such as data centers, cell towers, and fiber networks, in a deal valued at $4 billion.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: The 27 best new apps of 2025
item.description: As always, many of this year’s best apps are ones you’ve probably never heard of. Sure, there are some big names on this list, particularly in the buzzy field of artificial intelligence, but the real standouts of 2025 innovated on a smaller scale.
item.dc:creator: Jared Newman, Fast Company

item.title: React2Shell: Anatomy of a max-severity flaw that sent shockwaves through the web
item.description: The React 19 library for building application interfaces was hit with a remote code vulnerability, React2Shell, about a month ago. However, as researchers delve deeper into the bug, the larger picture gradually unravels.
item.dc:creator: Info World
